what&nbsp;<b>Choices</b>&nbsp;is and how it affects code generation&nbsp;<i>today</i>." style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;spacingLeft=10;spacingRight=10;" </mxCell> <mxCell id="tQl9TI6B8Drq-oMKitGQ-112" value="<b>ImplementationType</b>&nbsp;dictates whether the code generator should produce a standalone executable <i>program</i> or a software intended to be used by others (i.e., a&nbsp;<i>library</i>). The difference amounts to generating a `Main`&nbsp;module&nbsp;(that interacts with system IO to receive input and write output) or not." style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;spacingLeft=10;spacingRight=10;" vertex="1" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1"> <mxGeometry x="1627" y="510" width="273" height="120" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="tQl9TI6B8Drq-oMKitGQ-114" value="" style="edgeStyle=orthogonalEdgeStyle;rounded=0;orthogonalLoop=1;jettySize=auto;html=1;" edge="1" id="tQl9TI6B8Drq-oMKitGQ-113" value="The third option captures preference for how "data" (variables) should be organized and declared in the output software artifacts." style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;spacingLeft=10;spacingRight=10;" vertex="1" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1"> <mxGeometry x="908" y="760" width="258" id="tQl9TI6B8Drq-oMKitGQ-124" style="edgeStyle=orthogonalEdgeStyle;rounded=0;orthogonalLoop=1;jettySize=auto;html=1;exitX=0.5;exitY=0;exitDx=0;exitDy=0;entryX=1;entryY=0.5;entryDx=0;entryDy=0;" value="<b>Structure</b>&nbsp;captures if a group of variables should be organized into a single cohesive unit (likely a module, record, or class). For example, `inputStructure` declares if input variables should be organized into a single cohesive unit or not." style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;spacingLeft=10;spacingRight=10;" vertex="1" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1"> <mxGeometry x="600" y="565" width="210" height="125" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="tQl9TI6B8Drq-oMKitGQ-125" style="edgeStyle=orthogonalEdgeStyle;rounded=0;orthogonalLoop=1;jettySize=auto;html=1;" edge="1" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" source="tQl9TI6B8Drq-oMKitGQ-120" target="tQl9TI6B8Drq-oMKitGQ-64"> <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="tQl9TI6B8Drq-oMKitGQ-130" style="edgeStyle=orthogonalEdgeStyle;rounded=0;orthogonalLoop=1;jettySize=auto;html=1;exitX=0;exitY=0.5;exitDx=0;exitDy=0;entryX=0.193;entryY=0.994;entryDx=0;entryDy=0;entryPerimeter=0;startArrow=block;startFill=1;endArrow=none;" edge="1" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" source="tQl9TI6B8Drq-oMKitGQ-120" target="tQl9TI6B8Drq-oMKitGQ-127"> <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry"> <mxPoint x="80" y="809.7142857142858" as="targetPoint" /> </mxGeometry> </mxCell> <mxCell id="tQl9TI6B8Drq-oMKitGQ-120" value="<b>ConstantRepr</b>&nbsp;dictates preference for using (generated language) constant-defining keywords. If&nbsp;<i>Const</i>&nbsp;is declared, then the code generator will prefer using special "constant" keywords." style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;spacingLeft=10;spacingRight=10;" vertex="1" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1"> <mxGeometry x="150" y="760" width="210" height="100" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="tQl9TI6B8Drq-oMKitGQ-126" value="<b>ConstantStructure&nbsp;</b>declares whether the generated program should have constants:&nbsp;<i>inlined</i>,&nbsp;<i>placed with the input variables</i>, or placed in a separete module along (<i>Bundled</i>) or merely left as floating variables passed around each function." style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;spacingLeft=10;spacingRight=10;" vertex="1" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1"> <mxGeometry x="150" y="600" width="210" height="125" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="tQl9TI6B8Drq-oMKitGQ-127" value="<b>Note:&nbsp;</b>Not all languages support "const" keywords (e.g., Python supports neither deep nor shallow "const" but Java supports shallow "const" -- i.e., no re-assignment via `final`)." style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#fff2cc;strokeColor=#d6b656;spacingLeft=10;spacingRight=10;" vertex="1" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1"> <mxGeometry x="40" y="460" width="210" height="100" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="tQl9TI6B8Drq-oMKitGQ-129" style="edgeStyle=orthogonalEdgeStyle;rounded=0;orthogonalLoop=1;jettySize=auto;html=1;exitX=1;exitY=0.5;exitDx=0;exitDy=0;entryX=-0.004;entryY=0.923;entryDx=0;entryDy=0;entryPerimeter=0;" edge="1" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1" source="tQl9TI6B8Drq-oMKitGQ-126" target="tQl9TI6B8Drq-oMKitGQ-60"> <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="tQl9TI6B8Drq-oMKitGQ-132" value="<div>The fourth option declares two idea maps:</div><div>1. how (mathematical/<b>Space</b>) variable types should be translated to (code/<b>CodeSpace</b>) variable types.<br>2. how general (math) "concepts" (currently limited to Pi with a design only suitable for variables referenced) should be translated into (code) "concepts."</div>" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;spacingLeft=10;spacingRight=10;" vertex="1" parent="WIyWlLk6GJQsqaUBKTNV-1"> <mxGeometry x="906" y="870" width="270" value="<b>Note:&nbsp;</b>The design related to "ConceptMath" currently only supports replacing defining an instance of a translated "Pi" (the mathematical concept defined by 3.14159... at the Drasil level) into code with references to the generated language's concept of "Pi."" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#fff2cc;strokeColor=#d6b656;spacingLeft=10;spacingRight=10;" vertex="1" value="The fifth option declares extra "optional features," including documentation generation, logging settings, and auxiliary files to generate." rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(218, 232, 252), rgb(29, 41, 59)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(108, 142, 191), rgb(92, 121, 163));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 398px; height: 1px; padding-top: 450px; margin-left: 798px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.div: Choices captures user preferences for code generation (a configuration for the generator). Defining a Choices record means declaring a list of demands for the code generator of a specific system.

path.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(218, 232, 252), rgb(29, 41, 59)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(108, 142, 191), rgb(92, 121, 163));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 218px; height: 1px; padding-top: 530px; margin-left: 888px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: The first option is the target language(s) for generation.

path.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));
fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(218, 232, 252), rgb(29, 41, 59)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(108, 142, 191), rgb(92, 121, 163));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 248px; height: 1px; padding-top: 635px; margin-left: 873px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: The second option declares demands for a specific "architecture" for the software artifacts based on two switches: Modularity and ImplementationType. i.e., Modular/Unmodular and Program/Library.

path.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));
fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(218, 232, 252), rgb(29, 41, 59)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(108, 142, 191), rgb(92, 121, 163));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 188px; height: 1px; padding-top: 665px; margin-left: 1598px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: Modularity dictates whether the program is generated as a series of source code files or a single file.

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(218, 232, 252), rgb(29, 41, 59)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(108, 142, 191), rgb(92, 121, 163));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 398px; height: 1px; padding-top: 95px; margin-left: 798px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: We will discuss what Choices is and how it affects code generation today.

path.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(218, 232, 252), rgb(29, 41, 59)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(108, 142, 191), rgb(92, 121, 163));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 251px; height: 1px; padding-top: 550px; margin-left: 1598px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: ImplementationType dictates whether the code generator should produce a standalone executable program or a software intended to be used by others (i.e., a library). The difference amounts to generating a `Main` module (that interacts with system IO to receive input and write output) or not.

path.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(218, 232, 252), rgb(29, 41, 59)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(108, 142, 191), rgb(92, 121, 163));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 236px; height: 1px; padding-top: 770px; margin-left: 879px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: The third option captures preference for how "data" (variables) should be organized and declared in the output software artifacts.

path.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));
fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(218, 232, 252), rgb(29, 41, 59)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(108, 142, 191), rgb(92, 121, 163));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 188px; height: 1px; padding-top: 608px; margin-left: 571px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: Structure captures if a group of variables should be organized into a single cohesive unit (likely a module, record, or class). For example, `inputStructure` declares if input variables should be organized into a single cohesive unit or not.

path.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));
fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(218, 232, 252), rgb(29, 41, 59)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(108, 142, 191), rgb(92, 121, 163));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 188px; height: 1px; padding-top: 790px; margin-left: 121px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: ConstantRepr dictates preference for using (generated language) constant-defining keywords. If Const is declared, then the code generator will prefer using special "constant" keywords.

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(218, 232, 252), rgb(29, 41, 59)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(108, 142, 191), rgb(92, 121, 163));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 188px; height: 1px; padding-top: 643px; margin-left: 121px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: ConstantStructure declares whether the generated program should have constants: inlined, placed with the input variables, or placed in a separete module along (Bundled) or merely left as floating variables passed around each function.

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(255, 242, 204), rgb(40, 29, 0)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(214, 182, 86), rgb(109, 81, 0));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 188px; height: 1px; padding-top: 490px; margin-left: 11px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: Note: Not all languages support "const" keywords (e.g., Python supports neither deep nor shallow "const" but Java supports shallow "const" -- i.e., no re-assignment via `final`).

path.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(218, 232, 252), rgb(29, 41, 59)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(108, 142, 191), rgb(92, 121, 163));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 248px; height: 1px; padding-top: 910px; margin-left: 877px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.div: The fourth option declares two idea maps:

1. how (mathematical/Space) variable types should be translated to (code/CodeSpace) variable types.
2. how general (math) "concepts" (currently limited to Pi with a design only suitable for variables referenced) should be translated into (code) "concepts."

path.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(255, 242, 204), rgb(40, 29, 0)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(214, 182, 86), rgb(109, 81, 0));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 231px; height: 1px; padding-top: 1075px; margin-left: 238px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: Note: The design related to "ConceptMath" currently only supports replacing defining an instance of a translated "Pi" (the mathematical concept defined by 3.14159... at the Drasil level) into code with references to the generated language's concept of "Pi."

path.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

div.div: The fifth option declares extra "optional features," including documentation generation, logging settings, and auxiliary files to generate.

display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: The sixth option declares how strict "Physical" and "Software" constraints should be upheld: warnings or exceptions/panics.

path.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(218, 232, 252), rgb(29, 41, 59)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(108, 142, 191), rgb(92, 121, 163));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 248px; height: 1px; padding-top: 1280px; margin-left: 873px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: The seventh option declares methods for solving specific ODE problems (related to the SRS) using external libraries.

path.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(218, 232, 252), rgb(29, 41, 59)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(108, 142, 191), rgb(92, 121, 163));

display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: The eighth option is a mapping function from "internal concepts" (internal to the code generator) to their respective names that would appear in generated code.

div.div:
The ExtLib name is a bit deceiving. I was expecting it to be purely about libraries, not about how they will be used as well (i.e., for ODE solutions).
Note: Each ODELibPckg is language-specific packet that declares how ODEInfos can be translated into a series of steps for solving ODEs.

div.div: Note: A lot of these options are fairly self-explanatory, but the DocConfig is a bit more specific about documentation style: it is about Doxygen. So, the comment style prefers Doxygen-style formatted comments. For the languages that Doxygen doesn't support (e.g., Julia), common conventions are used.
